text: Here's what science actually says about confounding variables. Confounding variables can skew your results, misleading researchers and the public. Imagine a study linking coffee to heart disease. What if stress, not coffee, was the real culprit? Research shows that failing to account for confounding variables can lead to false conclusions. Being aware of these variables helps scientists create more accurate studies. Curious about more science insights? Follow for more!
